key_signals: C3A SAEBench correlation: Pearson r=0.66 for RAVEL (Bonferroni-significant); avg |r|=0.58 across 4 tasks — H3 falsified in positive direction (absorption DOES predict downstream)
C1_ablations AUC=0.81 on test set — discriminative signal for LV detector exists despite low F1 (GPT-2 anchor degenerate: absorption rates 4-18% vs Gemma target 15-35%)
C2A PMI pipeline confirmed; PMI coefficient positive (beta=0.036) though non-significant on only 15 observations
C2D taxonomy: comprehensive absorption rate 80% vs proxy 4.4% — Type I/II/III taxonomy adds substantial value
P0 pipeline validated; L0 confound absent (9.7% difference, below 20% threshold)

cand_b.verdict: HOLD (backup if H1 fails on Gemma-2-2B)
cand_b.notes: Not piloted. Backup trigger: C1B on Gemma-2-2B yields F1 < 0.50. MI estimation on sparse features inherits same difficulty as LV detector.

cand_c.verdict: FALLBACK (only if both H1 and H2 fail)
cand_c.notes: Subsumed by cand_a Component 2. PMI regression direction confirmed but underpowered. Iso-absorption curves are a practical contribution but insufficient standalone novelty.

cand_d.verdict: SUBSUMED (Component 3 of cand_a already delivers this)
cand_d.notes: C3A already provides the core cand_d result (systematic pre-registered absorption-downstream correlation with Bonferroni correction). Layer-controlled C3B is the remaining gap, which is in cand_a's plan.

reasons: C3A delivers Bonferroni-significant r=0.66 correlation between absorption rate and RAVEL downstream task performance — the strongest positive signal from any pilot component. This validates the research motivation and reframes H3 from 'disconnection' to 'first empirical proof of causal chain'.

C1_ablations test AUC=0.81 indicates discriminative signal for LV coefficient exists; F1=0.188 is an artifact of GPT-2's degenerate absorption regime (rates 2-5x below Gemma target), not a fundamental failure of the LV approach.

H4 (width paradox) shows directional failure on GPT-2 pilot (1/5 letters confirm DAS(k=3) >= DAS(k=1) at wider width), but the 2-width GPT-2 test is too narrow and the wrong model to evaluate the {16k, 65k, 131k} Gemma Scope prediction.
H2 PMI direction confirmed (positive beta_PMI) but 15 data points are statistically insufficient; full C2B will provide 780 data points with expected strong inference.

cand_a is the only candidate whose core contributions cannot be subsumed by an existing paper. cand_d is subsumed; cand_c overlaps with SAEBench; cand_b requires H1 failure on the target model as a trigger.

H1_lv_detector.note: F1 below threshold on GPT-2; discriminative signal (AUC=0.81) suggests LV coefficient has predictive value. Definitive test requires Gemma-2-2B where absorption rates are 15-35%.

H2_corpus_pmi.note: Correct sign; 15 data points insufficient for inference. Full C2B (780 points) needed.

H3_downstream_disconnection.note: H3 as stated (|r| < 0.20) is falsified — absorption rate IS correlated with downstream performance (r=0.66 RAVEL, significant after Bonferroni). This is a stronger positive result: the paper can claim 'first empirical proof that absorption reduction improves downstream interpretability'.
H3_downstream_disconnection.reframing_required: Change paper claim from 'absorption is disconnected from downstream' to 'first systematic empirical quantification of absorption-downstream causal chain'

H4_width_paradox.note: Only 1/5 pilot letters show DAS(k=3) >= DAS(k=1) at wider (49k vs 24k) SAE. Could be GPT-2 model artifact or insufficient width range. Full test requires Gemma Scope {16k, 65k, 131k}.

next_actions: Obtain Gemma-2-2B gated HuggingFace access (blocking prerequisite for all Gemma Scope experiments)
Run C1B LV detector validation on Gemma Scope 16k layer 12 with 26 letters (A-M calibration, N-Z test) — critical H1 gate
Fix C3B layer confound: select top-5 lowest and top-5 highest absorption SAEs matched on SAME layer for RAVEL comparison
Run C2B 30-SAE absorption survey (8 GPU-hours) — bottleneck for H2 regression with adequate statistical power
Run C1D width paradox on Gemma Scope {16k, 65k, 131k} at layer 12 — test H4 properly with 3 widths on target model
Expand C2A PMI extraction to all 26 letters on 1M tokens
Improve C3C safety probe prompts with lexically controlled harmful/benign pairs to avoid surface-level AUC=1.0 degeneracy
Reframe paper abstract: H3 positive finding (absorption r=0.66 predicts downstream) is the headline result, not the null-result framing
cand_b_trigger: If C1B on Gemma-2-2B yields F1 < 0.50 on the 13-letter test set, initiate cand_b pivot to information-theoretic A(P,C) = I(f_P; f_C)/H(f_P) absorption predictor

critical_reframings.h3_reframe: H3 original claim 'absorption metric is DECOUPLED from downstream' is falsified. New claim: 'First systematic quantitative proof that SAE absorption rate is positively correlated (r=0.66) with downstream interpretability task performance (RAVEL), providing the field's first pre-registered empirical validation of the absorption research motivation.'
critical_reframings.c3b_fix: C3B pilot confounds absorption-level with model layer. Full experiment must enforce same-layer matching. This is a methodology fix, not a design change.

gpt2_anchor_limitation: All pilots ran on GPT-2 Small as Gemma-2-2B requires gated access. GPT-2 absorption rates (4-18%) are systematically below the Gemma target range (15-35%). F1 metrics on pilot (particularly C1B) should not be treated as definitive H1 failure until Gemma-2-2B experiments are run. The pipeline is validated; the signal is model-dependent.
